channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Georgian government officials raid American think tank staff members' homes
item.description: Georgian government officials raided the homes of local staff of an American think tank who work on tracking Russian disinformation, raising alarm among prodemocracy activists, civil society groups and some U.S. officials ahead of Georgia’s Oct. 26 parliamentary elections.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Putin name-checks Trump at BRICS summit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin name-checked former President Trump at this week’s BRICS summit and seemingly welcomed the Republican nominee’s desire to end the Russian-Ukraine war. “What Mr.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: North Koreans training in Russia are now in combat zone: Ukraine Defense Intelligence
item.description: North Korean forces have entered the combat zone between Russian and Ukrainian forces for the first time after training at Kremlin military bases, Ukraine’s defense intelligence service said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Navalny widow to Americans: Don't take democracy for granted
item.description: Yulia Navalnaya, the widow of the late Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny, encouraged Americans not to take democracy for granted, in an interview on ABC’s “The View” that aired Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Mozambique's ruling party candidate declared winner of presidential election as rigging claims swirl
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's ruling party candidate Daniel Chapo was declared the winner of the country's presidential election Thursday as the opposition claimed the vote was rigged and one party said it would challenge the result in court.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey struck suspected Kurdish militant targets in Syria and Iraq for a second day on Thursday following an attack on the premises of a key defense company that killed at least five people, the state-run news agency reported.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian lawmakers ratify pact with North Korea as US confirms that Pyongyang sent troops to Russia
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian lawmakers on Thursday ratified a pact with North Korea envisioning mutual military assistance, a move that comes as the U.S. confirmed the deployment of 3,000 North Korean troops to Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Train carrying 55 people derails on Norway's north coast, killing at least 1 person and injuring 4
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — At least one person was killed and four people injured when a train running along Norway’s northern coast derailed Thursday with 55 people on board, police said.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A lawmaker from the far-right Freedom Party is elected as speaker of Austria's parliament
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — A lawmaker from the far-right Freedom Party was elected Thursday as the new speaker of Austria's parliament, weeks after the party won a national election for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US announces $135M in humanitarian assistance to Palestinians
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken on Thursday announced $135 million in new humanitarian assistance for Palestinians and said American negotiators will meet with Israeli and Qatari counterparts to revive talks on a cease-fire despite uncertainty over Hamas’s participation.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: In Colombia, Amazon River's extreme drought falls hard on Indigenous communities
item.description: LETICIA, Colombia (AP) — Marciano Flores stood knee-deep in the Amazon River with the rest of his crew, ready to haul in a giant net as a man in a canoe circled in an attempt to drive fish their way.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Indonesia says its coast guard drove away Chinese ship that interrupted survey in disputed sea
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian patrol ships drove a Chinese coast guard vessel away from a survey vessel in a disputed area of the South China Sea for the second time in three days, Indonesian authorities said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: EDNA TARIGAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin ends BRICS summit that sought to expand Russia's global clout but was shadowed by Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has presided at the closing session of a summit of the BRICS bloc of developing economies, hailing its role as a counterbalance to the West.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Well-known Cambodian investigative journalist released on bail
item.description: TA KHMAU, Cambodia (AP) — A Cambodian investigative reporter who was charged with a criminal offense after exposing online scams and corruption was released on bail Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US confirms North Korea has sent 3,000 troops to Russia for training and possible Ukraine combat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. said Wednesday that 3,000 North Korean troops have been deployed to Russia and are training at several locations, calling the move very serious and warning that those forces will be “fair game” if they go into combat in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, LOLITA C. BALDOR and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trash carried by a North Korean balloon again falls on the presidential compound in Seoul
item.description: South Korea says trash carried by a North Korean balloon has fallen on the presidential compound in central Seoul.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Turkey strikes Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria following deadly attack on defense company
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — Turkey’s air force struck Kurdish militant targets in Iraq and Syria on Wednesday in an apparent retaliation for an attack at a key state-run defense company that killed five people and wounded more than 20 others.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Some Liberal lawmakers ask Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau not to run for a fourth term
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Some lawmakers in Prime Minister Justin Trudeau's own Liberal Party asked the leader not to run for a fourth term Wednesday, handing him one of the biggest tests of his political career.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's population has fallen by 10 million since Russia's invasion, UN says
item.description: The United Nations said Ukraine's population has decreased by around a quarter, or 10 million people, since Russia's invasion of the country in February 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US, Israel warn of potential attacks on Sri Lankan tourist spots
item.description: United States and Israeli authorities warned their citizens on Wednesday against traveling to certain popular tourist spots in Sri Lanka, after receiving “credible” threats of an impending attack. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Explosion at Turkish aerospace facility ruled a terror attack
item.description: Turkish authorities say an explosion at a defense aerospace facility on Wednesday was a terrorist attack that killed an unknown number of people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Group of metal detectorists uncover hoard of coins in England
item.description: A group of metal detectorists discovered a hoard of 21 coins near Okehampton Castle nearly a year ago, but the news of their finding was just recently unveiled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon chief: North Korean troops are in Russia
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Lloyd Austin said North Korean troops appear to be in Russia. “We are seeing evidence that there are North Korean troops that have, that have gone to … Russia,” Austin told reporters in Rome on Wednesday. “What exactly they’re doing, left to be seen.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump campaign files FEC complaint over Labour staff campaigning for Harris
item.description: Former President Trump’s campaign team filed a complaint with the Federal Election Commission on Monday, labeling the U.K. Labour Party’s efforts to support Vice President Harris as “election interference.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Police bust restaurant serving ‘best-selling’ pizza with side of cocaine
item.description: German police have raided a Düsseldorf pizzeria for allegedly serving pies with a side of cocaine. The restaurant's 36-year-old manager was arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walmart employee found dead inside walk-in oven at Canada store: police
item.description: Authorities in Canada are continuing an investigation into the death of a Walmart employee who was found inside a walk-in oven at the store.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Woman becomes stuck upside down between boulders for 7 hours while trying to retrieve dropped phone
item.description: A woman who became trapped in between two boulders in Australia after falling into a crevice while trying to retrieve her phone has been saved, NSW Ambulance says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy says a US green light on a NATO invitation will sway Germany
item.description: Ukraine's president is hoping allies will take a more positive stance on his “victory plan” after the U.S. election.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel strikes Hezbollah naval base in Beirut as Iran rallies Gulf Arab nations
item.description: Hezbollah launched missiles on central Israel Tuesday. Meanwhile, Israel Defense Forces said it struck a Hezbollah naval base in Beirut, along with other terrorist targets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China holds live-fire drills near Taiwan, in another show of force
item.description: China's military is conducting live-fire drills around islands near Taiwan Tuesday that are being tracked by the Taiwan Defense Ministry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Peru’s ex-president gets 20+ years in prison in case linked to corruption scandal
item.description: LIMA, Peru (AP) — Peru’s former President Alejandro Toledo on Monday was sentenced to 20 years and six months in prison in a case involving Brazilian construction giant Odebrecht, which became synonymous with corruption across Latin America, where it paid millions of dollars in bribes to government
item.dc:creator: FRANKLIN BRICEÑO and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing student in Spain was likely crushed, incinerated after passing out in trash while partying: cops
item.description: A student missing in Majorca, Spain, was likely crushed to death in a garbage truck before she was incinerated at a waste disposal plant, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China holds live-fire drills opposite Taiwan week after large-scale exercise
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China is holding live-fire drills off the coast of its southern Fujian province facing Taiwan, just a week after a massive air-and-sea drill it described as punishment for Taiwan's president rejecting Beijing's claims of sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reddit user discovers mysterious 'doorway' in Antarctica
item.description: "Monsters Inc." bedroom doors, and an underground bunker were more suggested explanations, some in jest others not.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: NHC monitoring Hurricane Oscar as it nears Cuba
item.description: Hurricane Oscar is being closely monitored by the National Hurricane Center as it makes its way toward Cuba on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Japan’s ruling party HQ attacked with firebombs; man arrested: Reports
item.description: A man threw several firebombs into the headquarters of Japan’s ruling party in Tokyo Saturday, media outlets report.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama/The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cuba’s grid goes offline with massive blackout after a major power plant fails
item.description: HAVANA (AP) — Cuba’s electrical grid went down Friday after one of the island’s major power plants failed, a day after a massive blackout swept across the Caribbean island and with no official estimate for when service will be restored.
item.dc:creator: ANDREA RODRÍGUEZ, Associated Press, WGN 9
